programme.title: Stochelo Rosenberg & Peter Beets
programme.desc: The treasured compositions of guitarist Django Reinhardt, the founding father of hot club jazz, are engraved in everyone's memory. Although Reinhardt missed two fingers of his left hand, his virtuoso technique was unrivalled. Dutch guitarist Stochelo Rosenberg and pianist Peter Beets now have the pleasure to bring Django's music back to life with their infectious improvisations.

programme.title: Wes Montgomery
programme.sub-title: Herbie Man & Sonny Sharrock - Memphis underground
programme.desc: This selection of unforgettable performances by some of the most iconic and influential jazz guitarists in history showcases these legends' unique essence. Revel in Wes Montgomery's virtuosity as he performs the captivating “Nica's Dream” in Holland in 1965, and delight in the expressive beauty of his rendition of “Here's That Rainy Day”, recorded in London in that same year. Experience the timeless appeal of Joe Pass's “You Stepped Out of a Dream” and “You Turned The Tables on Me”, recorded live in Hanover in 1975. The interplay between Jim Hall and Atilla Zoller borders on the impossible as they perform “Extensions” in Hanover in 1973. Enjoy Jim Hall's lyrical solo performance of “I'm Getting Sentimental Over You” from 1964. Witness Sonny Sharrock's avant-garde fusion of jazz and rock in an exhilarating performance of “Peanut", recorded live in France in 1970. Sharrock is joined by flutist Herbie Mann in a soulful performance of “Memphis Underground” from 1969. Immerse yourself in the magic of jazz guitar!

programme.title: B.B.King
programme.sub-title: B.B. King Orchestra - The North Sea Jazz
programme.desc: The North Sea Jazz Festival is the largest indoor music festival in the world, known globally as the event where the past, present and future of jazz are featured within three days. Next to a firm base of jazz as the festival's staple music genre, many others, such as blues, soul, funk, or hip hop, pass by. Back in 1979, legendary American blues guitarist B.B. King gave an unforgettable performance for the audience in The Hague. Born in 1925 in a small town in Mississippi, 'Lucille' (as he nicknamed his guitar) helped him escape a miserable existence picking cotton. He arrived in Memphis in the late 1940s, where his cousin Bukka White taught him the tricks of the blues. Soon he developed his own style. King's virtuoso, narrative solos with lots of vibrato, have been an inspiration for most (rock) guitarists after him.

programme.title: Bill Evans, Chuck Israels & Larry Bunker
programme.sub-title: Bill Evans' European Tour: Live in '64, '65 & '70
programme.desc: Bill Evans: Live in '64, '65 & '70 features five separate performances by one of the most revered pianists in music history. This collection covers an 11-year span, highlighting Evans' ever-deepening artistry as he performs with four different all-star rhythm sections, including the stunning duo of bassist Niels-Henning Ørsted Pedersen and drummer Alan Dawson. This recording includes Evans' renditions of the rarely performed “Sareen Jurer,” “Blue Serge”, and “Twelve Tone Tune Two.” Among the many surprises in this collection is a live performance of “My Melancholy Baby” with saxophonist Lee Konitz.

programme.title: Dizzy Gillespie
programme.desc: An extraordinary collection of clips in all jazz genres from the most beautiful concerts and the most special festivals, performed by the finest jazz musicians.

programme.title: Miles Davis
programme.desc: American trumpeter Miles Davis (1926-1991) was a pioneering jazz musician from the late 1940s to the late 1980s. His influence on 20th century music is hard to overstate, as he trailblazed bebop, cool jazz, hardbop, modal jazz, and electronic music. On October 11, 1964, Miles Davis performed at Teatro Dell'Arte in Milan. With tenor saxophonist Wayne Shorter, pianist Herbie Hancock, bassist Ron Carter and drummer Tony Williams, Davis performed Autumn Leaves, My Funny Valentine, All Blues, All of You, and Joshua.

programme.title: Coleman Hawkins
programme.desc: A jazz festival named after the inventor of the instrument most associated with the genre, Adolphe Sax, would be incomplete without the man who laid the groundwork for how the instrument is played today. This must have been the reasoning of the organizers of the Festival International de Jazz Adolphe Sax in Dinant, Belgium, when they invited tenor saxophone giant Coleman Hawkins to perform there in June 1962. Appearing with a group that includes the French pianist George Arvanitas, one-time Duke Ellington bassist Jimmy Woode, and expat drummer Kansas Fields, the musician also known as ‘Bean' and ‘Hawk' serves up an hour-long set of familiar standards bookended by Hank Jones' “Chant” and J. J. Johnson's “Wee Dot”.

programme.title: Soloists of the Lucerne Festival Orchestra, Lucas Macías Navarro, Stefan Dohr & Jonathan Wegloop
programme.desc: The soloists from the Lucerne Festival Orchestra present two septets in this engaging 2020 concert, recorded at Lucerne Culture and Congress Center (KKL), Switzerland. It opens with Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart's Divertimento No. 11 in D major, K. 251, known as the ‘Nannerl-Septet'. Mozart composed it in 1776, probably on the occasion of his sister Maria Anna's name day, whose nickname was ‘Nannerl'. The septet is scored for oboe, two horns, two violins, viola and double bass. The program continues with Ludwig van Beethoven's Septet in E-flat major, Op. 20, which is scored for clarinet, horn, bassoon, violin, viola, cello and double bass. This 1799 piece achieved great popularity during Beethoven's lifetime. It was arranged for many various instrumental ensembles, including versions for two guitars, and piano four-hands, but also a trio for clarinet (or violin), cello, and piano, arranged by Beethoven himself.

programme.title: Gewandhausorchester Leipzig
programme.desc: Cinematic classical clips and energizing concert pieces featuring up-and-coming artists.

programme.title: Leonardo García Alarcón, Cappella Mediterranea & Mariana Flores
programme.sub-title: Vocal baroque works by Cavalli, Strozzi & Bembo
programme.desc: Argentinian conductor and harpsichordist Leonardo García Alarcón leads his ensemble Cappella Mediterranea in a program of Italian Baroque music. The ensemble performs some of the finest Baroque pieces composed by Francesco Cavalli, a prominent composer in 17th-century Venice, and two of his famous students, Barbara Strozzi and Antonia Bembo. Argentinian soprano Mariana Flores presents the vocal works. On the program are ‘Mira questi due lumi' from Cavalli's Le nozze di Teti e di Peleo, ‘Dimmi, Amor, che farò' from L'Oristeo, ‘Sinfonia della notte' from L'Egisto, and ‘E vuol dunque Ciprigna' from Ercole Amante ‘M'ingannasti in verità' from Bembo's Produzioni armoniche consacrate a Luigi XIV Strozzi's Sino alla morte, Che si può fare, Lagrime mie, L'amante segreto, and è Pazzo il moi core Biagio Marini's La Romanesca Tarquinio Merula's Aria Sopra La Cieccona and Dario Castello's Sonata Seconda. This performance was recorded at the magnificent Église Notre-Dame of Sablé-sur-Sarthe, France, on August 26, 2020.

programme.title: Neeme Järvi
programme.desc: Cinematic classical clips and energizing concert pieces featuring up-and-coming artists.

programme.title: Les Arts Florissants & Paul Agnew
programme.desc: Italian composer Claudio Monteverdi (1567-1643) composed nine books of madrigals in half a century, which helped transform the genre from the polyphonic, a cappella madrigals of the late Renaissance to the ‘concertato' madrigals of the early Baroque, shifting the style's focus to the ability of music to express emotions contained in a text. In 2011, British tenor Paul Agnew and renowned Baroque ensemble Les Arts Florissants started recording eight books of Monteverdi's madrigals. In this program, Agnew leads Les Arts Florissants in the madrigals of Book I. Published on New Year's day of 1587, Book I consist of seventeen madrigals for five voices. This performance was recorded at the Cité de la musique in Paris, France, in 2011.

programme.title: Martha Argerich, Evgeny Kissin, Iddo Bar-Shai & Arielle Beck
programme.sub-title: Martha Argerich – A Birthday Celebration - I
programme.desc: On the occasion of her 80th birthday, Argentinian pianist Martha Argerich explored chamber music repertoire in this wonderful concert, recorded at Château de Chantilly, France. The ‘Grande Dame' of the piano is joined by various renowned artists, including pianists Evgeny Kissin, Iddo Bar-Shaï, and Arielle Beck violinists Gidon Kremer, Maxim Vengerov, and Tedi Papavrami cellist Mischa Maisky, Flugelhorn player Sergei Nakariakov, as well as her daughter and grandson: Lyda Chen-Argerich (piano and viola) and David Chen-Argerich (piano). On the program are Alfred Schnittke's Congratulatory rondo for piano and violin Preludes No. 5, 21, and 24 from Mieczysław Weinberg's 24 Preludes, Op. 100 (arranged for solo violin by G. Kremer) ‘Waltz' and ‘Slava' from Sergei Rachmaninoff's 6 Morceaux, Op. 11, and Romance for six hands Fritz Kreisler's Liebesleid Robert Schumann's Fantasiestücke Op. 73, and Piano Quintet in E-flat major, Op. 44 and ‘Laideronnette, impératrice des Pagodes' from Maurice Ravel's Ma mère l'Oye. This performance was recorded on June 11, 2021.

programme.title: Tuva Semmingsen, Klaus Tönshoff, Danish National Symphony Orchestra & DR Big Band
programme.desc: Musicians of the Danish National Symphony Orchestra, the DR Big Band, and the Danish National Vocal Ensemble join forces under the baton of Klaus Tönshoff in this dazzling concert featuring great songs from black and white cinema. Norwegian mezzosoprano Tuva Semmingsen sings ‘Diamonds Are A Girl's Best Friend', from Gentlemen Prefer Blondes, ‘My Favorite Things' from The Sound of Music, ‘Over the Rainbow' from The Wizard of Oz, ‘Moon River' from Breakfast at Tiffany's, and many other unforgettable hits. This concert performance was recorded at the DR Koncerthuset in Copenhagen, Denmark, in June 2020.

programme.title: Protest Songs
programme.desc: Protest songs were a union between the emerging Folk music of the late 50s and early 60s, and the Civil Rights movement. They needed songs, which could be sung on marches, at causes, and could be anthems for the Civil Rights cause. And when Civil Rights wasn't so much achieved, but when it merged into the anti-war protest movement, then those Protest songs were also the soundtrack of that. The Vietnam War was seen as something, which was highly questionable. The new generation was thinking about that. So it was very much defining themselves against what had come before, against what was seen as an oppressive, hierarchical, patriarchal system, which had worked essentially during the war years, but didn't work anymore in the post-war years.

programme.title: Songs for the People
programme.desc: Protest songs about the conditions experienced in the 1920s and 1930s were written using traditional folk hillbilly/bluegrass traditions. Acts such as The Freedom Singers overthrowing 50s attitudes, bringing issues such as the Civil Rights Movement of the late 1950s and early 1960s into focus. This gave folk music a tremendous strength in the US creating such legends as Joan Baez, Woody Guthrie and Pete Seeger.

programme.title: Eric Clapton & Steve Winwood
programme.desc: History rocked and rock made history during a sold-out three-gig stand at Madison Square Garden in late February 2008 when icons Eric Clapton and Steve Winwood performed a concert together for the first time since their all-too-brief days in the legendary Blind Faith in 1969. Whether they will ever team again is unknown. But with Eric Clapton and Steve Winwood Live From Madison Square Garden fans everywhere are able to experience the sound and the glory of a performance on that famous stage by two of rock's greatest artists.

programme.title: El velo de novia
programme.desc: Después de descubrir una pintura perdida hace mucho tiempo, el curador del museo Avery y el nuevo miembro de la junta Peter investigan los orígenes de la obra de arte mientras planean una gala para revelarla. Protagonizada por Lacey Chabert, Kevin McGarry, Autumn Reeser y Alison Sweeney.

programme.desc: Harold y Kopus encuentran a Junior en Connecticut. Rachel asume el papel de madre cuando Jean pierde el control. Kopus defiende a la tribu de un ataque.

programme.title: Dark Winds
programme.desc: El policía tribal navajo Joe Leaphorn debe resolver crímenes junto a su nuevo ayudante Jim Chee, que vuelve a la reserva por primera vez desde que era niño. Ellos se enfrentarán a fuerzas malignas, terrenales y sobrenaturales.

programme.title: Dark Winds
programme.desc: Leaphorn y Chee entrevistan al testigo superviviente de los asesinatos del motel Big Rock. Chee se reúne con el forense. La causa de la muerte sigue siendo un misterio. Manuelito realiza un descubrimiento escalofriante.

programme.title: Dark Winds
programme.desc: La sobrina de Leaphorn celebra su Kinaaldá mientras Leaphorn recupera los cuerpos de las víctimas de asesinato. Manuelito y Chee investigan el paradero de la familia mormona desaparecida.

programme.title: Dark Winds
programme.desc: Leaphorn sospecha que todos los crímenes ocurridos en la reserva están relacionados. Leaphorn invita a cenar a Chee. Manuelito realiza un descubrimiento.

programme.title: Dark Winds
programme.desc: Mientras custodia a un sospechoso relevante, Leaphorn descubre que detrás de este crimen hay más de lo que hubiera imaginado. Manuelito se enfrenta a sus peores temores. Chee debe elegir entre su carrera y su pueblo.

programme.title: Dark Winds
programme.desc: Leaphorn, Chee y Manuelito descienden al escondite de la Sociedad Búfalo. Allá dentro, se enfrentan a unas duras decisiones que podrían cambiar el resto de sus vidas.

programme.title: La patrona
programme.desc: La señora Guerra disfruta de haber separado a su hijastra de Maximiliano. Verónica sabe que Valentina será la próxima víctima. Además, descubre que la abuela de la joven fue envenenada.

programme.title: La patrona
programme.desc: Alejandro trata de indagar la conexión de los Suárez con la mina del Chamuco y Ricardo se le une. La ira de Antonia cae sobre Cecilia. David no la defiende y Francisca la recibe en su casa.

programme.title: Al otro lado del muro
programme.desc: Mientras Joel oye por qué el forense se vio obligado a ocultar los documentos que exculpan a Sofía, Lorena los rompe. Ernesto sorprende Alejandra y ella simula estar ofendida.

programme.title: Al otro lado del muro
programme.sub-title: Joel le pide perdón a Sofía
programme.desc: El agente admite su error al darse cuenta de la inocencia de Sofía lloran juntos, se reconcilian y hacen el amor. Max interna a Jennifer en un psiquiátrico por sus incoherencias.

programme.title: Sin senos sí hay paraíso
programme.desc: La hija de la Diabla provoca a Catalina, mientras en el auto está Nachito. Aunque él de lejos logra ver al amor de su vida, recibe una nueva orden de Daniela. Yésica sigue hospitalizada.

programme.title: Sin senos sí hay paraíso
programme.desc: La Diabla piensa que Catalina no está muerta. Ella, aún hospitalizada, quiere encontrar al responsable del atentado que sufrió. El alcalde le dice que el sicario sobrevivió.

programme.title: Señora Acero
programme.desc: Sara y Berta pelean en un canje de rehenes y, cuando creen tener el control, se disparan las alarmas. Mario lanza su sistema de distribución con drones. Salvador va a Los Ángeles.

programme.title: Señora Acero
programme.desc: Salvador entra al túnel, pero lo que no sabe es que el Teca sigue sus pasos y podría ser usado como carnada para dar con su madre. Dóriga le confiesa su amor a Sara. La Tuti consuela a otro hombre.

programme.title: Corazón valiente
programme.desc: Luis molesta a Ángela en su trabajo, mientras Fernanda jura que la quitará de su camino. Samantha sigue cuidando a Willy, él está con otra mujer y ella no puede evitar estar celosa.

programme.title: Corazón valiente
programme.desc: Luis le dice a Fernanda que no puede matar a Ángela porque es su mujer. La amenaza diciéndole que, si le llega a hacer algo, él sacará a la luz pública la información sobre la muerte de los padres de Juan Marcos.

programme.title: Corazón valiente
programme.desc: Samantha y Willy vuelven a estar juntos. Luis le explica a Fernanda que será sumamente complicado enamorar a Ángela después de haber intentado matarla, pero eso a ella no le importa.

programme.title: Corazón valiente
programme.desc: Juan Marcos deja ver sus sentimientos por Ángela. Emma y Willy se presentan como las ovejas negras de la familia. Empujan a Isabel por las escaleras y tiene un aborto.

programme.title: Corazón valiente
programme.desc: Las cosas no son lo que parecen, pero Ángela pensará que hay algo entre Juan Marcos y Fernanda. Willy queda impresionado con las habilidades de Samantha como luchadora.

programme.title: Corazón valiente
programme.desc: Isabel cae en la tentación con Renzo y durante su encuentro hablan de Ángela, quien los ve haciendo el amor. La mujer de Juan Marcos rechaza a Diego y su esposo escucha todo.

programme.title: Corazón valiente

programme.title: Imperio de mentiras

programme.title: Teresa

programme.title: Teresa

programme.title: Teresa

programme.title: Exploring Europe's Great Food Markets
programme.desc: The Kapani market is situated just behind the Aristotelous square of the city of Thessaloniki, facing the Aegean Sea. Exploring this market is exploring the city and its history: a tasteful mix of Sephardic, Balkan, Turkish, and Greek delights. All can be found in the intricate streets of the market or tasted at the many cafés and tavernas operating there daily. (S4 E04)

programme.title: Exploring Europe's Great Food Markets
programme.desc: At Europe's biggest open air market, there is a world to discover! Torino's Porta Palazzo is a reverberating hub full of colors and people. Our journey takes us from the region's finest cherry trees that become the city's tastiest gelato to unearthing the region's finest cheeses and meat recipes! If you start to lick the screen don't panic... (S1 E04)

programme.title: Exploring Europe's Great Food Markets
programme.desc: Lying in the culinary heart of Vienna like an island, a little world of treats rises in the midst: the Naschmarkt food market. A throng of buyers and curious onlookers meander through the stalls all consumed by the scent of spices, distracted by the smell of sausage, and stunned by the diversity of turnips, tubers, foliage, and fruit on display. The Naschmarkt truly is Vienna's most popular belly. (S1 E05)

programme.title: Exploring Europe's Great Food Markets
programme.desc: The 'green' city of Germany has an incredible gothic cathedral with a food market at its feet. Every morning, six days a week, farmers from the Black Forest bring their trout and herb pestoes, wild berries, and smoked ham to the city. Be it a vegan wurst or a tasty cheesecake, here the surprises never finish! (S2 E02)

programme.title: Exploring Europe's Great Food Markets
programme.desc: The CentralTirgus is the biggest covered city food market in Europe. Just aside the Art Nouveau Old Town, we find century-old rye-bread making, grilled lampreys, soviet milk sausages, and hemp butter. Here the best of Latvian traditions are pushed fast-forward into the digital era by young and curious chefs, bloggers, and pop-up restaurants. (S2 E03)

programme.title: Exploring Europe's Great Food Markets
programme.desc: The pink city hosts a coffer full of world-famous food treasures. The Victor Hugo Market has black truffles of Quercy, Rocamadour cheese, foie gras, and white beans of the most traditional Cassoulet on sale. And Michelin-starred chef Michel Sarràn gives us his tips on what the belly of Toulouse really is. (S2 E04)

programme.title: Exploring Europe's Great Food Markets
programme.desc: The old ladies selling their baskets full of vegetables and cheese on the Dolac market in Zagreb bring us back in time. At the market, we go from the breathtaking Mediterranean islands to the Austrian Alps, from freshly caught hake and simple Baltic snacks to internationally awarded cheese and a unique Turkey breed. (S2 E05)

programme.title: It Ain't No Joke!
programme.desc: In this kick-off to the final season, the cast takes viewers back through the most outrageous, comical, and memorable repos of the series. And, get a sneak peek of the final season of Operation Repo.

programme.title: Hooligans and Explosions
programme.desc: Will a frying pan-wielding housewife stop Lou and Sonia from getting their car? Plus, a repo involving a truck filled with black market contraband goes horrifyingly wrong for Matt and Froy.

programme.title: Natural Liar
programme.desc: First, Froy and Ronnie dodge tomatoes and pesticide when an organic farmer won't give up his tractor, and when they are in the clear, Froy confronts Ronnie about telling Sonia about the boob purchase and inadvertently reveals the Lambo repo. Next, Carlos and Matt get an offer of stardom from a slick Hollywood agent. And finally, Sonia drags more information from Ronnie and doesn't understand why everyone's going behind her back with Froy's big Lambo repo. Plus, they lose control of a Chevy Impala while repoing from a mace wielding shopper.

programme.title: The Junior Partner
programme.desc: Now that Lou has accepted Matt's offer to buy into the company, the team is back in action, but there are still some unanswered questions. Sonia, Lyndah, and Froy interrupt a rich kid's brunch party to take away his ride, Matt and Lyndah attempt to repo an unusual vehicle from a man who thinks he's Uncle Sam, and Lou and Matt are in a losing match with an unruly gang of jacked-up rugby players.

programme.title: The Final Finale
programme.desc: On this special one hour series finale of Operation Repo, the team are scrambling to get ready for the huge Yacht repo to secure the sale of the company and the distribution of their severance packages, while still going after every day repos. In this episode, Sonia and Froy find a payment procrastinating pervert in the park, Matt and Ronnie put the brakes on a rich racecar driver, Matt and Carlos get stuck in the middle of sign maker's gluing process, and Ronnie and Carlos try to reason with an irresponsible retro racist. And finally, on their most dangerous repo ...

programme.title: Broken Bones and Broken Phones
programme.desc: First, Froy, Lou, and Matt wrestle a group of body builders when going after a Jeep Commander. Next, an unemployed stock broker gets a rude awakening when Lou and Lyndah wake him up inside his car. And finally, Froy learns why you should never text and not drive to pick up Sonia, because she breaks his phone in two.

programme.title: Missed Dates
programme.desc: First, Lyndah is put in a tough situation - she has to control a sobbing, cop-calling debtor and turn away Frankie's unwanted and inappropriate advances - all at the same time. Then, Matt and Froy have two problems on their hands, when they decide to haul away both a stripped-down husk of a car and the phone number of its owner's coquettish and bathrobe-wearing ex-girlfriend. Finally, Lou, Froy, and Lyndah head way out into the country to wrangle with a case of mistaken identity and a trio of shotgun-wielding cultists.

programme.title: T-Bone Trouble
programme.desc: The team's work with anger management starts to pay off - with disastrous consequences. Matt and Sonia get into a standoff with a wealthy divorcee, his daughter and her raging boyfriend.

programme.title: Now Serving Mrs. Pizarro
programme.desc: First, Matt and Froy discuss what they will do with their severance pay once the company is sold, but have to put discussion on hold while they repo repo a hovercraft golf cart from a snooty golfer. Next, Sonia and Ronnie's easy day at the salon gets disastrous when the should-be debtor serves Sonia with a lawsuit. And finally, Froy and Carlos head out to a worksite to repo the bulldozer that Carlos should have been training for.

programme.title: Naked and Dangerous
programme.desc: Sonia and Lyndah try to repossess a hot tub away from a group of upset partiers. Then, Lou and Matt try to negotiate with a pair of unintelligible and enraged parade participants. And, Lou and Lyndah must repo a car from a group of old men.

programme.title: First Rule of Repo
programme.desc: Froy and Sonia encounter a city employee who tries to give them the boot, and a mechanic's shady scheme comes crashing down on Matt and Ronnie. But with Froy still in debt to Sonia, an RO's offer to make a quick buck convinces Froy to break the first rule of repo.

programme.title: Instruction and Destruction
programme.desc: First, Sonia and Froy repossess an experimental concept motorcycle from a hose-wielding yuppie. Then, Froy and Sonia head to a casting agency to repossess a car from a two-faced receptionist with a special talent for four-letter words.

programme.title: Burning Up
programme.desc: Lou's new business relationship with Matt starts to unravel when a botched job puts a serious strain on the company.

programme.title: Pyramid Scams and Princesses
programme.desc: First, Lou, Sonia, and Matt wrangle with two belligerent truckers who will stop at nothing to protect their massive tractor-trailer. Next, a young girl learns that she's been a victim of a pyramid scam when Lyndah and Lou come for her car. Finally, Matt, Sonia, and Lyndah enjoy a little nightlife when they hit the club to take down an entitled princess who's high on charm, but late on her payments. But the debtor's not the only one who fights to get the car.

programme.title: BREEDER OF THE PACK
programme.desc: A breed on the border of extinction gets a helping hand to extend their 'leash' on life. Have you heard of the Dandie Dinmont Terrier? No? Join the club, not many people have. The Dandie is listed as an endangered purebred breed and only 300 puppies are born each year. While that is still a precarious position for the Dandie, they would have long ago been extinct had it not been for Mike Macbeth. Mike regularly makes semen runs for other breeders around the world to expand the lines and pedigrees. But that selection process, as well as deciding who she will sell pups to, is a bit like going for a high-level government security job - there's a background check, an intense interview and binding contracts going back and forth. Mike can't afford any missteps when it comes to breeding these dogs - literally, their survival depends on it.

programme.title: BREEDER OF THE PACK
programme.desc: Two sisters, same breed, one competition - which sibling will be the top dawg? Erin and Jen Braybrook are sisters. Sure, they love each other, but when it comes to the Olde English Bulldogges they breed, there's also some good old fashioned sibling rivalry. They regularly show their Bullies at rare breed shows and their goal is to win best in breed and be recognized as serious breeders. They're young, new to breeding, but are also determined to inject fun into the dog world. The sister duo also want to dispel the myth that Old English Bulldogges are non-agile slow pokes. Jen and Erin have entered their Bullies in an event that will have the dogs dock jumping, disc diving, and pulling weights. But like any competition, only one dog can win. First, will it be a Bullie? And second, will it be Jen's dog or Erin's dog? Which sister will end up being the 'top dawg'?

programme.title: The Wildlife Docs

programme.title: The Wildlife Docs
programme.desc: Chai, a critically endangered black rhino, must undergo a dental procedure that will require all The Wildlife Docs' participation. Plus, Lulu, a Flemish giant rabbit, is in the Animal Care Center to have a cyst removed. Find out if the docs can heal this hefty hare.

programme.title: The Wildlife Docs
programme.desc: Experience the continent of Africa, as we meet five spectacular species: the king of beasts, leaping lemurs, huge hippos, a clan of meerkats, and laughing hyenas - all need a visit from The Wildlife Docs.

programme.title: The Wildlife Docs
programme.desc: When a mother black bear and her two cubs wandered into populated areas looking for food and creating a dangerous situation, Busch Gardens was asked to help by caring for the cubs until they could be returned back into the wild. Today, the mother bear is going to her new home. Then, a flying fox named Kiwi was found with a broken hand. The Wildlife Docs must perform surgery before this mega-bat has a mega-problem.

programme.title: The Wildlife Docs
programme.desc: Mikey, a four-year-old male, red kangaroo, has stopped eating, and it's up to The Wildlife Docs to figure out why. Plus, the docs encounter an unusual animal when Alfalfa, a rock hyrax, comes in for his annual checkup.

programme.title: The Wildlife Docs

programme.title: The Wildlife Docs

programme.title: The Wildlife Docs

Executive produced by Director X and directed by Karen Chapman.

programme.title: After Skid Row
programme.desc: After Skid Row documents the journey of Barbie Carter as she navigates the transition to housed life following the brutal reality of a decade on the streets. Her unique story and compelling character help to both demystify and deeply personalize a homelessness epidemic that continues to swell across Los Angeles. Directed by Lindsey Hagen.

programme.title: Fight Or Flight
programme.desc: Fight or Flight chronicles the journey of the first female pilot employed by Colorado Parks and Wildlife. Drawn to flying as a means to flee her early trauma, she pursues a career in professional aviation and discovers she must first assemble the tools required to face her past and heal. Directed by Lindsey Hagen.

programme.desc: When a family cotton farm defies the conventions holding them back—they embark on a journey of transformation that radically shifts their farm, their family, and their future. Directed by Lindsey Hagen.

programme.title: NF Forward
programme.desc: Identical twins, Adam and Neil Pearson, are navigating life in South London with a rare genetic disorder, neurofibromatosis. Despite their identical DNA, the twins are affected in vastly different ways. The one thing that remains the same is their perspective of life and how to live it best. Directed by Jonathan Braue.

programme.title: NF Forward
programme.desc: Then a fateful moment for a family in North Carolina would change his life forever. Directed by Jonathan Braue.Maddox Gibson is a fun-loving 5th grader with a big smile who loves Legos and wants to be a chef. But in 2017, Maddox was a 10 year-old in China who had been living in an orphanage his entire life. Complicating his life even more was a tumor on his optic nerve caused by NF that had grown out of control, resulting in loss of sight and significant disfigurement of his left eye.

programme.title: NF Forward
programme.desc: Kylie suffers from a rare genetic disease called Neurofibromatosis which from birth affected her left eye. She has never been able to see from it, as she got older the plexiform tumors caused her eye to be removed. Against odds Kylie, a bubbly 16yr old is a high performing athlete, a back spotter in the Texas Elite Cheer team. While facing crippling mental blocks and battling low muscle tone because of her condition she preserves in a sport that favors not only physical aesthetics but requires precision, strength and flawless technique.

programme.title: NF Forward
programme.desc: Travis has lived in the fear of breaking his bones most of his life. From the age of 2 a tumor, caused by a rare disease called Neurofibromatosis, has been growing from his spine down his leg. Directed by Rory K. Mcharg.

programme.title: NF Forward
programme.desc: Aidan Fraser has undergone 20 major surgeries and been on five different chemotherapies in 17 years due to NF. Directed by Jamoody Jaafar.

programme.title: NF Forward

programme.title: Eat Flowers

programme.title: Planet 4K
programme.sub-title: South America - From Chile to Patagonia
programme.desc: The film takes you on a journey that begins deep in the rainforest and ends in Patagonia with its glaciers and snow-capped volcanoes. During the earth's formation, the South American continent evolved as the most autonomous of the continents. The area developed its own unique flora and fauna, jungles with unbelievable biodiversity, climbers and vines. Even today, the number of tropical plants in the region remains a mystery. A trip to South America is a glimpse into the past. The film takes you on a journey that begins deep in the rainforest and ends in Patagonia with its glaciers and snow-capped volcanoes.

programme.title: Turtle Paradise 1
programme.desc: Leave your worries from the world above behind and dive into a vibrant undersea world inhabited by the iconic sea turtles, for 2 full hours of Nature Relaxation. programme.title: Rainbow Reef Relaxation II 1
programme.desc: Immerse your senses on an unparalleled underwater journey to some of the world's most vibrant coral reefs and undersea paradises as seen in true 4K Ultra High Definition, paired to soothing new music by Relax Moods. Viewers will discover a plethora of stunning sea creatures, thanks to the incredible underwater cinematography by Peter Schneider filmed over the last several years.

programme.title: Morning Inspiration
programme.desc: Striking Abstracts S1E2: Cinematice Dimensions — Immerse yourself in a visual journey where shapes dance and colors converse, inviting you to redefine the way you perceive art. Dare to experience the extraordinary with soft and deep house beats.

programme.title: Morning Inspiration
programme.desc: Trending Art S1E1 — A curated collection of artwork from both emerging talents and established artists, meticulously handpicked by our expert curation team. The diverse array of artistic visions is a testament to creativity and the power of visual expression.

programme.title: Shades Of Color
programme.desc: Shades of Color S1E10: Shades Of Black — Explore the allure of darkness where black reigns supreme in a mesmerizing artistic journey that unveils the profound depth of the human experience and soft house meets beats with a dark dreamy edge.

programme.title: Shades of Color
programme.desc: Shades of Color S1E7: Shades of Purple — Celebrating the richness and diversity of purple, with vibrant hues and bold compositions that evoke a sense of regal elegance and mystery.

programme.title: Shades Of Color
programme.desc: Shades Of Color S1E13: Shades Of Gold — Experience the opulent allure of an art collection adorned with the lustrous elegance of gold. These works illuminate creativity, from shimmering landscapes to gilded abstractions, embodying the richness of this resplendent hue.

programme.title: Shades of Color
programme.desc: Shades of Color: Shades Of Gold E13 — A luminous art collection celebrating fortune, warmth, and the rich glow of possibility. Spanning diverse mediums, each piece shimmers with golden hues, while the soundtrack infuses the experience with lively, mystical charm.

programme.title: Shades of Color
programme.desc: Shades of Color S1E2: Shades of Green — Explore a stunning art collection celebrating the color green. From lush landscapes to abstract works, this collection showcases the versatility and serenity of green in a myriad of artistic expressions.

programme.title: Spring Forward: Spring Impressions
programme.desc: Spring Forward: Spring Impressions E3 — Celebrate the beauty of spring through the luminous brushstrokes of Impressionism. Featuring works by contemporary artists & old masters, the art is accompanied by contemporary classical music, immersing viewers in the colors & rhythms of the season.
